name: fzf (Fuzzy Finder for Everything)

performance.notes: Go-based; handles millions of lines with <1ms per keystroke

agent_readiness.notes: General-purpose fuzzy finder. Pipe any list, get interactive selection. Shell integration: Ctrl+R (history), Ctrl+T (file), Alt+C (directory). --preview for live previews. --multi for multi-select. --bind for custom keybindings. Use in shell scripts: `git branch | fzf`. fzf.vim, fzf-tab, fzf-lua plugins. Python, Go, Rust libraries wrap fzf. Extremely fast even on huge lists. Standard tool in developer dotfiles. bat/delta/tree for preview content.

gotchas: Interactive TUI — not suitable for non-interactive scripts
Not a library (invoke via subprocess from code)
best_when: Interactive selection from any list in shell scripts
History search, file navigation, git workflow
summary: fzf is a blazing-fast fuzzy finder for interactive selection from any list. Pipe any input: `git branch | fzf`. Shell integration: Ctrl+R history, Ctrl+T file, Alt+C directory. --preview for live content previews. --multi for multi-select. The standard terminal productivity tool — handles millions of lines with sub-millisecond response. Ubiquitous in developer dotfiles.
